questions.question_text: The following compound is called:-
questions.question_html: The following compound is called:-
questions.explanation: The following compound is tertiary amine.

questions.question_text: The following amine is called as
questions.question_html: The following amine is called as
questions.explanation: C 6 H 5 NH 2 is known as aniline. Here, the amino group is directly attached to phenyl ring so it is also known as phenylamine. This is an aromatic primary amine.

questions.question_text: (C 2 H 5 ) 2 CHNH 2 . The following amine can be classified as:
questions.question_html: (C2H5)2CHNH2. The following amine can be classified as:
questions.explanation: This is a primary amine because the Nitrogen of amine is only connected to one carbon.

questions.question_text: The following amine can be classified as (C 2 H 5 ) 2 NH
questions.question_html: The following amine can be classified as (C2H5)2NH
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is attached to two carbons, so this is a secondary amine.

questions.question_text: The following amine can be classified as
questions.question_html: The following amine can be classified as
questions.explanation: The degree of amine is represented by the number of carbon atoms directly attached to the N of the amino group. In the given structure, the N atom is directly attached to 3 carbons. Two carbons of -CH 3 group and 1 carbon of naphthalene ring.

questions.question_text: The following reaction takes place in the presence of:
questions.question_html: The following reaction takes place in the presence of:
questions.explanation: -NO 2 group is reduced to -NH 2 using H 2 /Pd.

questions.question_text: Nitro compounds are reduced by iron scrap and hydrochloric acid to yield one of the following compounds:
questions.question_html: Nitro compounds are reduced by iron scrap and hydrochloric acid to yield one of the following compounds:
questions.explanation: RNO 2 + Fe/HCl {tex}\to{/tex} RNH 2 Scrap iron with HCl acts as a reducing agent to reduce nitro group to the amine.

questions.question_text: For producing amines, the reaction of nitro compounds with iron scrap is preferred because:
questions.question_html: For producing amines, the reaction of nitro compounds with iron scrap is preferred because:

options.text: FeCl 2 is produced which undergo hydrolysis
options.html: FeCl<sub>2</sub> is produced which undergo hydrolysis

options.text: FeO is produced in the reaction
options.html: FeO is produced in the reaction

questions.correct_option_text: FeCl 2 is produced which undergo hydrolysis
questions.explanation: On reducing nitro compound by using scrap iron with HCl, FeCl 2 is produced which undergoes hydrolysis to produce HCl. Hence small amount of HCl is required to initiate the reaction. C 6 H 5 NO 2 + 3Fe + 6HCl {tex}\to {/tex} C 6 H 5 NH 2 + 3FeCl 2 + 2H 2 O

questions.question_text: Quaternary ammonium salt is formed:
questions.question_html: Quaternary ammonium salt is formed:

options.text: By nucleophilic substitution of tertiary amine
options.html: By nucleophilic substitution of tertiary amine

options.text: By nucleophilic substitution of primary amines
options.html: By nucleophilic substitution of primary amines

questions.correct_option_text: By nucleophilic substitution of tertiary amine
questions.explanation: The primary amine is obtained by nucleophilic substitution of an alkyl halide by NH 3 . NH 3 + R-X {tex}\to{/tex} R-NH 2 +HX This product then behaves as a nucleophile because of a lone pair of N of RNH 2 and further reacts with an alkyl halide to form secondary and tertiary amines, and finally quaternary ammonium salt. RH 2 + RX {tex}\to{/tex} R 2 NH + HX R 2 NH can further act as a nucleophile and react with RX R 2 NH + RX {tex}\to{/tex} R 3 N + HX R 3 N can further attack RX to give quaternary amine salts R 3 N + RX = R 4 N + X - The reaction cannot go any further because now the lone pair is not present on N and its valency is filled.

questions.question_text: The main product formed by treating an alkyl or benzyl halide with excess ammonia______.
questions.question_html: The main product formed by treating an alkyl or benzyl halide with excess ammonia______.
questions.explanation: The major product is the primary amine. NH 3 + R-X {tex}\to{/tex} RNH 2 +HX If NH 3 is added in excess then the reaction will not go any further and primary amine will be a major product.

questions.question_text: Hinsberg’s reagent reacts with primary and secondary amines to form sulphonamides. This reagent is also known as
questions.question_html: Hinsberg’s reagent reacts with primary and secondary amines to form sulphonamides. This reagent is also known as
questions.explanation: C 6 H 5 SO 2 Cl 2 .This is benzene sulphonyl chloride known as hinsberg reagent. It is used to distinguish between primary, secondary, and tertiary amine.

questions.question_text: p – toluenesulphonyl chloride does not react with
questions.question_html: p – toluenesulphonyl chloride does not react with
questions.explanation: This is because there is no hydrogen in tertiary amine to be substituted by sulphonyl group.

questions.question_text: p – toluenesulphonyl chloride is used to:
questions.question_html: p – toluenesulphonyl chloride is used to:

options.text: as a replacement of benzenesulphonyl chloride
options.html: as a replacement of benzenesulphonyl chloride

options.text: for the separation of a mixture of amines
options.html: for the separation of a mixture of amines

options.text: for the distinction of primary, secondary and tertiary amines
options.html: for the distinction of primary, secondary and tertiary amines

questions.explanation: p – toluenesulphonyl chloride can also be used instead of benzene-sulphonyl chloride to distinguish between 1 0 , 2 0 , 3 0 amines. Also, the product with a primary amine is soluble in alkali, the product with secondary amine is insoluble in alkali and tertiary amine does not give any reaction, therefore we can isolate and distinguish between primary, secondary, and tertiary amines.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following shows the carbylamine reaction in the presence of heat?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following shows the carbylamine reaction in the presence of heat?

options.text: CH 3 =CH 2 + H-I {tex}\to{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 I + CH 3 CHICH 3
R-NH 2 + C 6 H 5 COCl {tex}\to{/tex} R-NHCOC 6 H 5 + HCl
CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 I + NaCl {tex}\to{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 -Cl + NaI

questions.explanation: In carbylamines reaction, primary amine reacts with chloroform and base to form isocyanide having a foul smell.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their basic strength: C 6 H 5 NH 2 , C 2 H 5 NH 2 , (C 2 H 5 ) 2 NH 2 , NH 3 .
questions.question_html: Arrange the following in decreasing order of their basic strength: C6H5NH2, C2H5NH2, (C2H5)2NH2 , NH3.

options.text: ​C 6 H 5 NH 2 > C 2 H 5 NH 2 > (C 2 H 5 ) 2 NH 2 > NH 3
(C 2 H 5 ) 2 NH 2 > C 2 H 5 NH 2 > NH 3 > C 6 H 5 NH 2
C 2 H 5 NH 2 > (C 2 H 5 ) 2 NH 2 > NH 3 > C 6 H 5 NH 2 .

questions.correct_option_text: (C 2 H 5 ) 2 NH 2 > C 2 H 5 NH 2 > NH 3 > C 6 H 5 NH 2
questions.explanation: Alkyl amines and ammonia are more basic than aniline because in aniline lone pair on N is in resonance with benzene ring so is less available. Also, secondary alkyl amines are more basic than primary amine and ammonia due to +I effect of the alkyl group which increases basicity.

questions.question_text: The nitration reaction of amines can be controlled in order to obtain the desired para nitro derivative as the major product by:
questions.question_html: The nitration reaction of amines can be controlled in order to obtain the desired para nitro derivative as the major product by:

options.text: Reacting with a desired nitro compound
options.html: Reacting with a desired nitro compound

questions.explanation: The activating power of aniline can be controlled by acetylation of -NH 2 group by acetic anhydride or acetyl chloride to protect the -NH 2 group. This will give the para nitro product as the major product, instead of o,m,p mixture that we get if we do direct nitration of aniline. C 6 H 5 NH 2 + CH 3 COCl {tex}\to{/tex} C 6 H 5 NHCOCH 3 C 6 H 5 NHCOCH 3 + conc HNO 3 + H 3 O + {tex}\to{/tex} p-nitroaniline (major) + o-nitroaniline (minor)

questions.question_text: Which of the following respond to the isocyanide test?
questions.question_html: Which of the following respond to the isocyanide test?
questions.explanation: Only primary amines undergo carbylamine reaction (isocyanide test) because only they can form isocyanide with chloroform and alc.KOH. RNH 2 + CHCl 3 + 3KOH {tex}\times{/tex} RNC (foul smell) + 3KCl + 3H 2 O

questions.question_text: Which one of the following behaves like Lewis base?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following behaves like Lewis base?
questions.explanation: Amines behave as Lewis base because of the presence of lone pair on N, whereas in amides and cyanides the lone pair on nitrogen is involved in resonance. In esters, the lone pair on oxygen is involved in resonance.

questions.question_text: The geometry of amines is_____.
questions.question_html: The geometry of amines is_____.
questions.explanation: An amine molecule has the shape of a somewhat flattened triangular pyramid, with the nitrogen atom at the apex. An unshared electron pair is localized above the nitrogen atom.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of tertiary amine?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is an example of tertiary amine?
questions.explanation: Benadryl is a tertiary amine as it has a nitrogen connected to 3 carbon atoms directly. Adrenaline and ephedrine are secondary amine while novocain is primary amine.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is used as an anaesthetic in dentistry?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is used as an anaesthetic in dentistry?
questions.explanation: Novocaine (or Novocain), also known as procaine hydrochloride, is a local anaesthetic that is commonly used during dental procedures, like cavity fillings.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is used to increase blood pressure?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is used to increase blood pressure?
questions.explanation: Ephedrine is a stimulant used to prevent low blood pressure during spinal anaesthesia. It increases heart rate and blood pressure, expands bronchial tube, and increases body heat.

questions.question_text: Diazonium salts are used in the preparation of
questions.question_html: Diazonium salts are used in the preparation of
questions.explanation: Diazonium salt on reaction with other aromatic compounds produces dyes.

questions.question_text: Which of the following amines is most soluble in water?
questions.question_html: Which of the following amines is most soluble in water?
questions.explanation: Because of hydrogen bonding with water, ethylamine is highly soluble in water.

questions.question_text: The heating of Quaternary amines gives Hoffmann product. It proceeds via the ______ pathway.
questions.question_html: The heating of Quaternary amines gives Hoffmann product. It proceeds via the ______ pathway.
questions.explanation: Quaternary ammonium hydroxide salts undergo an E 2 elimination, because of stability of transition state. The major product formed is always less substituted alkene. The less highly substituted alkene is sometimes referred to as the Hoffman product.

questions.question_text: Heating a(n) ____ results in a Cope elimination.
questions.question_html: Heating a(n) ____ results in a Cope elimination.
questions.explanation: Heating of Amine oxide result in cope elimination which is cis/syn elimination. The Cope reaction or Cope elimination, is an elimination reaction of the N-oxide of a tertiary amine to form an alkene and a hydroxylamine. ​​​​​​​

questions.question_text: Secondary amines react with the nitrosonium ion to generate:
questions.question_html: Secondary amines react with the nitrosonium ion to generate:
questions.explanation: Secondary amines react with nitrosonium ion to form N-nitrosoamine. CH 3 -NH-CH 3 {tex}\xrightarrow[{HCl}]{{NaN{O_2}}}{/tex} (CH 3 ) 2 -N-N=O

questions.question_text: The nitrogen atom of trimethylamine is __________ hybridized which is reflected in the CNC bond angle of __________.
questions.question_html: The nitrogen atom of trimethylamine is __________ hybridized which is reflected in the CNC bond angle of __________.
questions.explanation: The N atom uses its one 2s and three 2p orbitals for sp 3 hybridization and the bond angle is 108° less than normal tetrahedral bond angle due to lone pair- bond-pair repulsion which is more than bond pair-bond pair repulsion.

questions.question_text: The following compound is called:
questions.question_html: The following compound is called:
questions.explanation: This is pyrrole, a heterocyclic compound.

questions.question_text: Name the following amine as per the IUPAC system
questions.question_html: Name the following amine as per the IUPAC system
questions.explanation: The IUPAC name of is 2-bromo-N - methylaniline.

questions.question_text: Which of the following amines can be resolved into enantiomers?
questions.question_html: Which of the following amines can be resolved into enantiomers?
questions.explanation: 2-pentamine will have a chiral centre, therefore it can be resolved into enantiomers. CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 C*HCH 3 NH 2 The C* in 2-pentan amine is the chiral center.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of secondary amine?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is an example of secondary amine?
questions.explanation: N of N – ethyl – 1 – propanamine is bonded to two carbon atoms, therefore it is a secondary amine.

questions.question_text: When pentanal reacts with ethylamine under conditions of acid catalysis, the major organic product is _______.
questions.question_html: When pentanal reacts with ethylamine under conditions of acid catalysis, the major organic product is _______.
questions.explanation: This will yield imine. Imines are typically prepared by the condensation of primary amines and aldehydes and less commonly ketones: RNH 2 + R'C(O)R {tex}\to{/tex} RN=C(R')(R) + H 2 O

questions.question_text: Amines are soluble in:
questions.question_html: Amines are soluble in:

options.text: only slightly soluble in organic solvents
options.html: only slightly soluble in organic solvents

questions.explanation: Amines are soluble in water due to hydrogen bonding with water.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following compounds in order of increasing boiling point: CH 3 NHCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 OCH 2 CH 3 ; (CH 3 ) 3 N; and CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 OH.
questions.question_html: Arrange the following compounds in order of increasing boiling point: CH3NHCH2CH3; CH3OCH2CH3; (CH3)3N; and CH3CH2CH2OH.

options.text: Low to high: CH 3 NHCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 OH.​​​​​​; (CH 3 ) 3 N​​​​​​​; ​​​​​​​CH 3 OCH 2 CH 3 .​​​​​​​
Low to high: CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 OH​​​​​​; CH 3 NHCH 2 CH 3 ; (CH 3 ) 3 N​​​​​​​; ​​​​​​​CH 3 OCH 2 CH 3 .​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
Low to high; CH 3 OCH 2 CH 3 .​​​​​​; (CH 3 ) 3 N; CH 3 NHCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 OH.​​​​​​​
Low to high: (CH 3 ) 3 N ; CH 3 OCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 NHCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 OH.

questions.correct_option_text: Low to high: (CH 3 ) 3 N ; CH 3 OCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 NHCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 OH.
questions.explanation: The increasing order of boiling point is as follows: Low to high: (CH 3 ) 3 N; CH 3 OCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 NHCH 2 CH 3 ; CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 O. This is on the basis of intermolecular interactions.

questions.question_text: When a 1° amine reacts with an alkyl sulfonyl chloride, the major organic product is __________.
questions.question_html: When a 1° amine reacts with an alkyl sulfonyl chloride, the major organic product is __________.
questions.explanation: Sulphonyl chloride reacts with the primary amine to form sulphonamide. RNH 2 +R I SO 2 Cl {tex}\to{/tex} R I SO 2 NHR + HCl

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a secondary amine _
questions.question_html: Which of the following is a secondary amine _
questions.explanation: This is secondary amine because nitrogen is connected to 2 carbon atoms directly.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compound give dye test?
questions.question_html: Which of the following compound give dye test?
questions.explanation: Azo-dye test is given by primary aromatic amines. Primary aromatic amines react with nitrous acid to form diazonium salt which undergoes a coupling reaction with beta-naphthol to give orange dye.

questions.question_text: Sec – Butylamine is the common name of which compound?
questions.question_html: Sec – Butylamine is the common name of which compound?
questions.explanation: Sec – Butylamine is the common name of 2-butanamine (CH 3 CH 2 CHNH 2 CH 3 ).

questions.question_text: Arrange the following in order of increasing basicity: aniline, p – nitroaniline, p – toluidine, and p – methoxyaniline.
questions.question_html: Arrange the following in order of increasing basicity: aniline, p – nitroaniline, p – toluidine, and p – methoxyaniline.

options.text: p – methoxyaniline p – nitroaniline < aniline < p – toluidine
options.html: p – methoxyaniline p – nitroaniline < aniline < p – toluidine

options.text: p – nitroaniline < aniline< p – methoxyaniline < p – toluidine
options.html: p – nitroaniline < aniline< p – methoxyaniline < p – toluidine

options.text: aniline < p – methoxyaniline p – nitroaniline < p – toluidine
options.html: aniline < p – methoxyaniline p – nitroaniline < p – toluidine

options.text: p – nitroaniline < aniline < p – toluidine < p – methoxyaniline
options.html: p – nitroaniline < aniline < p – toluidine < p – methoxyaniline

questions.correct_option_text: p – nitroaniline < aniline < p – toluidine < p – methoxyaniline
questions.explanation: -OMe group at a para position will increase the basicity of more than-CH 3 group at the para position. While the presence of –NO 2 at a para position will decrease the basicity.

questions.question_text: Pyridine typically undergoes________ electrophilic aromatic substitution as compared to benzene.
questions.question_html: Pyridine typically undergoes________ electrophilic aromatic substitution as compared to benzene.
questions.explanation: Pyridine is less reactive than benzene towards electrophilic aromatic substitution because nitrogen is more electronegative than carbon and acts as an electron-withdrawing group.

questions.question_text: In Pyridine, the preferred site of nucleophilic substitution is one of the following positions:
questions.question_html: In Pyridine, the preferred site of nucleophilic substitution is one of the following positions:
questions.explanation: Due to resonance, this position is electron rich and steric hindrance will be the least.

questions.question_text: The major product of the reaction between m-dinitro benzene with NH 4 HS is ______.
questions.question_html: The major product of the reaction between m-dinitro benzene with NH4HS is ______.
questions.explanation: In the presence of NH 4 HS, one nitro group of dinitrobenzene gets reduced and gives m- nitroaniline as a major product.

questions.question_text: Aniline does not undergo one of the following:
questions.question_html: Aniline does not undergo one of the following:
questions.explanation: The F.C. alkylation and F.C. acylation reaction take place in the presence of anhydrous AlCl 3 , which is a Lewis base as it is electron deficient, it attacks the lone pair on nitrogen in aniline and forms an insoluble complex which precipitates out and reaction does not happen further. C 6 H 5 NH 2 + AlCl 3 {tex}\to{/tex} C 6 H 5 NH 2 + AlCl 3

questions.question_text: Benzene diazonium chloride reacts with phenol in which the phenol molecule attack para position of phenol to form p – hydroxyazobenzene. This reaction is called________.
questions.question_html: Benzene diazonium chloride reacts with phenol in which the phenol molecule attack para position of phenol to form p – hydroxyazobenzene. This reaction is called________.
questions.explanation: In Coupling reaction, benzene and phenol get coupled through -N=N- linkage. The compounds containing this type of linkage are called azo compounds. C 6 H 5 N 2 + Cl - + C 6 H 5 OH {tex}\to{/tex} p - C 6 H 5 N=N C 6 H 4 OH (dye)

questions.question_text: The reaction of Benzene diazonium chloride with aniline yields
questions.question_html: The reaction of Benzene diazonium chloride with aniline yields

options.text: mixture of ortho and para – aminoazobenzene
options.html: mixture of ortho and para – aminoazobenzene

questions.explanation: This is a coupling reaction in which two benzene derivatives are held by -N=N- linkage resulting in the formation of an azo compound. C 6 H 5 N 2 + Cl - + C 6 H 5 NH 2 → C 6 H 5 N=N C 6 H 4 NH 2 This is azo dye test.

questions.question_text: Anisole reacts with a mixture of concentrated sulphuric and nitric acids to yield a mixture of ortho and para nitroanisole
questions.question_html: Anisole reacts with a mixture of concentrated sulphuric and nitric acids to yield a mixture of ortho and para nitroanisole

options.text: ortho and para in equal amounts
options.html: ortho and para in equal amounts

options.text: ortho and para in unequal amounts
options.html: ortho and para in unequal amounts

questions.explanation: In p-nitroanisole, the lone pair on oxygen is in conjugation with the pi bond of the benzene ring. Due to resonance, the nucleophilic centers are created at ortho and para positions. -OCH 3 hence is an activating group and hence o/p activating. The NO 2 + ion can attack at ortho and para positions, but since steric hindrance is least at para position, the major product is p-nitroanisole.

questions.question_text: In a coupling reaction, the azo products obtained, involve:
questions.question_html: In a coupling reaction, the azo products obtained, involve:
questions.explanation: Due to their positive charge, diazonium cations may participate in an electrophilic aromatic substitution as an electrophile. The electrophilic reaction center is the terminal nitrogen of the -N=N + group. As a result, two aromatic compounds are coupled by a -N=N- group. This is known as the azo group (diazo group). The corresponding reaction is called diazonium coupling (diazo coupling, azo coupling). However, the electrophilicity of diazonium ions is only relatively weak, as their positive charge is delocalized.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following when reacts with NaOH, the product is sodium benzoate?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following when reacts with NaOH, the product is sodium benzoate?
questions.explanation: Benzoic acid reacts with NaOH to form sodium benzoate, this is a neutralization reaction where acid reacts with a base to give salt and water. C 6 H 5 COOH + NaOH {tex}\to{/tex} C 6 H 5 COO - Na + + H 2 O

questions.question_text: Esterification is the reaction of one of the below compounds with alcohol:
questions.question_html: Esterification is the reaction of one of the below compounds with alcohol:
questions.explanation: Carboxylic acids react with alcohol in acidic medium to form sweet smelling compounds called esters, this reaction is called esterification reaction. CH 3 COOH + CH 3 OH + H + {tex}\to{/tex} CH 3 COOCH 3 + H 2 O

questions.question_text: The molecular formula of ethyl acetate is:
questions.question_html: The molecular formula of ethyl acetate is:
questions.explanation: Its molecular formula is C 4 H 8 O 2 and its chemical formula is CH 3 COOC 2 H 5 .

questions.question_text: When methylamine reacts with HCl, the product is________.
questions.question_html: When methylamine reacts with HCl, the product is________.
questions.explanation: Due to the presence of lone pair on nitrogen, methylamine acts as a Lewis base and reacts with HCl, H + ion from HCl forms an adduct (salt) methyl ammonium chloride, CH 3 NH 3 + Cl - .

questions.question_text: Direct nitration of aniline yields a significant amount of meta derivative. To obtain more p – nitro derivative, one or more of the below can be done_________.
questions.question_html: Direct nitration of aniline yields a significant amount of meta derivative. To obtain more p – nitro derivative, one or more of the below can be done_________.
questions.explanation: Direct nitration of aniline yield significant amount of meta derivative, this is because the use of HNO 3 during nitration of aniline causes the formation of anilinium ion(C 6 H 5 NH 3 + ). Anilinium ion is responsible for the formation of meta nitro aniline. To prevent this, the initial reaction of aniline with acetic anhydride acetylates -NH 2 group. C 6 H 5 NH 2 + CH 3 COOCOCH 3 {tex}\to{/tex} C 6 H 5 NHCOCH 3 . Now, -NHCOCH 3 is an activating group, which on nitration followed by hydrolysis form para nitro aniline as a major product.

questions.question_text: The below reaction yields Yields the following The 1, 2, 3 that are involved in completing the above synthesis are
questions.question_html:
The below reaction yields

Yields the following

The 1, 2, 3 that are involved in completing the above synthesis are
questions.explanation: It is direct nitration of aniline using a nitrating mixture which gives a mixture of products. The optimum temperature required is 288K.

questions.question_text: Aniline does not undergo Friedel – Crafts reaction because:
questions.question_html: Aniline does not undergo Friedel – Crafts reaction because:

options.text: Anilium ion deactivates any further reaction
options.html: Anilium ion deactivates any further reaction

questions.explanation: AlCl 3 being a lewis acid reacts with the lone pair of -NH 2 group of aniline forming an adduct (C 6 H 5 NH 2 + AlCl 3 ) which deactivates the benzene system hence no friedal craft reaction occurs.

questions.question_text: The base hydrolysis of an ester is called______.
questions.question_html: The base hydrolysis of an ester is called______.
questions.explanation: Base hydrolysis of ester produces the salt of carboxylic acid and alcohols as the product, and soaps are salts of carboxylic acids. Therefore, this reaction is called a saponification reaction.

questions.question_text: The nitrogen's lone pair in pyrrolidine is best described as occupying what type of orbital?
questions.question_html: The nitrogen's lone pair in pyrrolidine is best described as occupying what type of orbital?
questions.explanation: Pyrrolidine is tetrahydropyrrole. The nitrogen atom in pyrollidine is sp 3 hybridized. Two sp 3 hybridized orbitals are involved in pairing with carbon, one sp 3 hybridized orbital is involved in pairing with hydrogen and one sp 3 hybridized orbital is occupied by a lone pair.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following reagents is most suitable in completing the following synthesis? {tex}R - \mathop {\mathop C\limits^{||} }\limits^O - N{H_2} \to R - N{H_2}{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Which one of the following reagents is most suitable in completing the following synthesis?
{tex}R - \mathop {\mathop C\limits^{||} }\limits^O - N{H_2} \to R - N{H_2}{/tex}
questions.explanation: Conversion of amide to amine having one carbon less is known as Hoffmann bromide reaction. RCONH 2 + Br 2 + 4NaOH → RNH 2 + Na 2 CO 3 + 2NaBr + 2H 2 O

questions.question_text: Gabriel synthesis is used for the preparation of:
questions.question_html: Gabriel synthesis is used for the preparation of:
questions.explanation: In Gabriel Phthalimide reaction, the sodium or potassium salt of phthalimide is N-alkylated with a primary alkyl halide to give the corresponding N -alkylphthalimideis for producing primary amines. This is because of the reaction of sodium or potassium salt of phthalimide with alkyl halide impure SN 2 reaction.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following cannot be obtained by Gabriel phthalimide synthesis?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following cannot be obtained by Gabriel phthalimide synthesis?
questions.explanation: In Gabriel's phthalimide reaction, the potassium salt of phthalimide is formed. It reacts readily with the primary alkyl halide to form the corresponding alkyl derivative. But aryl halide (C 6 H 5 X) does not react with potassium salt of phthalimide. Because C-X bond in haloarene (alkyl halide) is difficult to be cleaved due to a partial double-bond character. Hence, do not undergo SN 2 reaction with potassium salt of phthalimide. So, aromatic primary amines cannot be prepared by Gabriel phthalimide synthesis.
